title: aphragmus oxycarpus var. glaber
intro: Aphragmus oxycarpus var. glaber, also known as Sea Cress, is a plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is commonly found in coastal areas. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Sea Cress have been used in herbal medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, aiding in wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In herbal preparations, Sea Cress is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
